assertions.text: The driver was accelerating while traveling eastbound (speed was increasing while moving east).
The vehicle's travel direction was eastbound at the time of the event.
The vehicle was struck from behind (rear-ended) by another vehicle.
The driver applied the brakes right as the rear impact happened.
Acceleration was occurring before the rear impact.
Braking began at approximately the same time as the impact (braked right as it happened).
There was one distinct impact (a single rear-end collision).
No explicit severity claim was made (severity not specified).

verdicts.evidence: [speed increased from 5.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 35.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3) and [compute_stats reports a change of 30.0 km/h over -5 to 0 s](T2).
The telemetry provides speed magnitude but no vehicle-heading/GPS bearing channel; direction (eastbound) cannot be determined from available channels and is therefore unverifiable.

EDR crash-pulse shows a longitudinal delta-V with signed peak [ -18.0 km/h at t=142.5 ms](T1) indicating a rear impact on this vehicle as recorded by EDR summary [max_delta_v_long_kmh -18.0 at 142.5 ms](T1) and detect_events reports an impact delta-V magnitude of [18.0 km/h](T4).
Brake channel shows a transition from 0 to 1 at [t=-0.5 s](T2) with raw samples showing brake off at [-1.0 s]=0.0 and on at [-0.5 s]=1.0 (and at [0.0 s]=1.0)(T3).

Speed was increasing prior to impact from [5.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 35.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3), showing acceleration before the impact; accelerator pedal samples also show nonzero values up to [t=-0.5 s]=0.0% then dropped to [0.0% at t=-0.5 s](T3).

Brake application began at approximately [t=-0.5 s](T2) which is immediately before impact at [t=0 s] (impact time defined as t=0 in window_info)(T1), consistent with braking starting right as the impact happened.
EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded=1](T1) and detect_events found a single crash-pulse delta-V event with [magnitude 18.0 km/h](T4).

The narrative makes no numeric severity claim; telemetry shows EDR summary lists [max_delta_v_long_kmh -18.0](T1) but the assertion states no severity was specified — that absence is a non-quantitative fact and thus supported by the lack of a severity value in the claim.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle was accelerating before impact ([5.0 km/h to 35.0 km/h](T3)), a brake application occurred at [t=-0.5 s](T2) just prior to impact ([t=0 s](T1)), and the EDR records a single rear-oriented crash pulse with [18.0 km/h](T4) longitudinal delta-V, so the narrative is consistent with the sensor data.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
